commands.review_summary:
The PR changes gateway handshake pairing policy to silently pair fresh Control UI WebChat browser-origin clients with valid shared auth again, updates gateway auth regression tests, and rewrites the changelog note for https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/81289.

Reproducibility: yes. From source inspection of the PR diff and current call site, the patched helper would allow Control UI WebChat browser-origin `not-paired` handshakes into the silent auto-approval path, and current tests show device-token sessions survive shared-token rotation.
commands.review_followup: Human security/product review is needed because the PR reopens browser-origin silent pairing before generation-bound browser device tokens are implemented, and the external PR still lacks real behavior proof.

target.title: Restore Control UI gateway token pairing [AI]
target.body:
## Summary

- Restore valid gateway-token Control UI browser access for fresh local browser devices.
- Keep browser-origin upgrades and non-Control browser-origin pairing on the explicit approval path.
- Update the gateway auth regression tests and changelog wording for the #81289 UX regression.

## Context

#81289 prevented all browser-origin clients from silent local pairing. That blocked the durable-device-token path from the security report, but it also made a fresh Control UI browser with a valid gateway token require explicit pairing.

This PR restores the previous Control UI UX in a narrower way: only fresh Control UI browser pairing is allowed. Role/scope/metadata upgrades from browser-origin sessions still require explicit pairing, and non-Control browser clients remain blocked from silent pairing.

The durable-token generation follow-up is still the longer-term security fix: auto-minted browser device tokens should be tagged with the shared gateway auth generation that created them, then rejected after gateway-token rotation.

## Verification

- `pnpm test src/gateway/server/ws-connection/handshake-auth-helpers.test.ts src/gateway/server.auth.browser-hardening.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `git diff --check`
